records.ety: [OE. & AS. ende; akin to OS. endi, D. einde, eind, OHG. enti, G. ende, Icel. endir, endi, Sw. ände, Dan. ende, Goth. andeis, Skr. anta. √208. Cf. Ante-, Anti-, Answer.]

defs: The extreme or last point or part of any material thing considered lengthwise (the extremity of breadth being side); hence, extremity, in general; the concluding part; termination; close; limit; as, the end of a field, line, pole, road; the end of a year, of a discourse; put an end to pain; -- opposed to beginning, when used of anything having a first part.
quotes: Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof.

defs: Point beyond which no procession can be made; conclusion; issue; result, whether successful or otherwise; conclusive event; consequence.
quotes: My guilt be on my head, and there an end.
O that a man might know The end of this day's business ere it come!

defs: Termination of being; death; destruction; extermination; also, cause of death or destruction.
quotes: Unblamed through life, lamented in thy end.
Confound your hidden falsehood, and award Either of you to be the other's end.
I shall see an end of him.

defs: The object aimed at in any effort considered as the close and effect of exertion; purpose; intention; aim; as, to labor for private or public ends.
quotes: Losing her, the end of living lose.
When every man is his own end, all things will come to a bad end.

defs: That which is left; a remnant; a fragment; a scrap; as, odds and ends.
quotes: I clothe my naked villainy With old odd ends stolen out of holy writ, And seem a saint, when most I play the devil.

defs: One of the yarns of the worsted warp in a Brussels carpet.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ended; p. pr. & vb. n. Ending.]

defs: To bring to an end or conclusion; to finish; to close; to terminate; as, to end a speech.
quotes: On the seventh day God ended his work.

defs: To form or be at the end of; as, the letter k ends the word back.
To destroy; to put to death.
To come to the ultimate point; to be finished; to come to a close; to cease; to terminate; as, a voyage ends; life ends; winter ends.
A combining form signifying within; as, endocarp, endogen, endocuneiform, endaspidean.
